message.content:
Repo: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-2; pass 1 already merged 22 fixes). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
Dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only), no metered API (subscription OAuth), engine imports no adapter/dataset (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; back findings with a concrete repro/PoC. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects, not style. Pass-1 findings #101-#122 are already FIXED — do not re-report them; DO report anything they missed or any regression they introduced.

Independent VERIFIER #2 of 2. A pass-2 assessor reported:
TITLE: README quickstart leads with `pip install assay-engine`, but the package is not published on PyPI (404)
DIMENSION: docs vs code drift / over-claims
SEVERITY: LOW
FILE: README.md:23-26; pyproject.toml:6 README.md:24
DESCRIPTION: The README Quickstart's first instruction is `pip install assay-engine` / `pip install "assay-engine[all]"` (L24-25), presented as the primary install path with no caveat. The distribution name is assay-engine (pyproject.toml:6) but it is not on PyPI — https://pypi.org/pypi/assay-engine/json returns HTTP 404. A user copy-pasting the quickstart cannot install the package; the only working path is an editable/source install (CONTRIBUTING.md correctly uses `pip install -e ".[dev]"`). The runnable example works from source (verified: `python examples/minimal_study.py` succeeds), so the methodology demo is fine — but the headline install command is non-functional for a production/public reader. For a Beta (Development Status :: 4) package this is a real onboarding over-claim.
SUGGESTED FIX: Either publish assay-engine to PyPI, or until then add a note in the Quickstart that the package is not yet on PyPI and show the from-source install (`pip install -e .` after cloning), matching CONTRIBUTING.md. At minimum mark the pip line as the post-publication path.
Independently determine if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the code; PoC where applicable. is_real=false if false positive / already handled / documented-justified scope. Recalibrate severity. Give evidence.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify this claim by examining the README, pyproject.toml, and checking PyPI.

content.content:
1	# hc-assay
2	
3	**A reusable blueprint for rigorous, reproducible empirical ML/NLP on security & privacy data.**
4	
5	`hc-assay` is not a single study. It is an **engine + a methodology** that you clone onto a
6	dataset to build an independent, empirical understanding of it — and, where the dataset
7	carries external expert-asserted claims, to adjudicate those claims against that
8	independent understanding.
9	
10	Status: **engine implemented and composed.** The dataset-agnostic engine (`src/assay_engine`)
11	is in place and hardened — the contracts, the methodology core (hypotheses, three verdicts, the
12	two firewalls, content-bound pre-registration, the measurement↔interpretation fence), all five
13	infrastructure seams (tiered reasoning, self-hosted observability, persistence, orchestration,
14	baseline toolkit), an append-only hash-chained provenance trail, and a single composed runner —
15	`run_study` — that ties them into one governed end-to-end flow (ADR-0010). The engine core is
16	dependency-free; heavy backends are lazy-imported behind optional extras (`reasoning`,
17	`observability`, `persistence`, `orchestration`, `baseline`, or `all`) so it installs, imports,
18	and unit-tests offline (ADR-0006). You build a study by implementing the adapter Protocols for
19	your dataset and calling `run_study`.
20	
21	## Quickstart
22	
23	```bash
24	pip install assay-engine            # dependency-free core
25	pip install "assay-engine[all]"     # + every optional backend
26	```
27	
28	```python
29	from assay_engine import StudyPlan, run_study, auto_approve
30	
31	# implement the adapter Protocols for your dataset (parser, baseline builder, the
32	# discover/confirm callables, an optional external-claims source), then:
33	plan = StudyPlan(definition=..., source=..., baseline_builder=..., authority=..., ...)
34	
35	result = run_study(plan, gate_handler=auto_approve)   # gate_handler is required
36	print(result.phases, result.discovery_verdicts, result.scorecard)
37	result.provenance        # append-only, hash-chained audit trail (verified before return)
38	```
39	
40	A complete, runnable example (both modes, synthetic data, no backends needed) is in
41	[`examples/minimal_study.py`](examples/minimal_study.py):
42	
43	```bash
44	python examples/minimal_study.py
45	```
46	
47	`gate_handler` is required so governance is a conscious choice: pass `auto_approve` to opt out
48	of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that can block/park before each confirmatory
49	step. See [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) for the adapter contract and
50	[docs/METHODOLOGY.md](docs/METHODOLOGY.md) for the method.
51	
52	---
53	
54	## What it does
55	
56	Given a security & privacy dataset, `hc-assay`:
57	
58	1. **Builds an independent empirical baseline** of the data using ML/NLP — deterministic,
59	   versioned, and reproducible by a hostile reviewer. This baseline is the one object in
60	   the system derived purely from the data.

1	[build-system]
2	requires = ["hatchling"]
3	build-backend = "hatchling.build"
4	
5	[project]
6	name = "assay-engine"
7	dynamic = ["version"]  # single-sourced from src/assay_engine/__init__.py:__version__ (#120)
8	description = "Dataset-agnostic engine for rigorous, reproducible empirical ML/NLP on security & privacy data."
9	readme = "README.md"
10	requires-python = ">=3.11,<3.15"
11	license = "Apache-2.0"
12	license-files = ["LICENSE"]
13	authors = [{ name = "thehipsterciso" }]
14	keywords = ["empirical", "reproducibility", "methodology", "security", "privacy", "ml", "nlp"]
15	classifiers = [
16	    "Development Status :: 4 - Beta",
17	    "Intended Audience :: Science/Research",
18	    "Intended Audience :: Developers",
19	    "Operating System :: OS Independent",
20	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3",
21	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
22	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.12",
23	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13",
24	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.14",
25	    "Topic :: Scientific/Engineering",
26	    "Topic :: Security",
27	    "Typing :: Typed",
28	]
29	# The engine core is dependency-free; heavy backends are optional extras (lazy-imported,
30	# ADR-0006). The composed runner and methodology work with no extras installed.
31	dependencies = []
32	
33	[project.urls]
34	Homepage = "https://github.com/thehipsterciso/hc-assay"
35	Repository = "https://github.com/thehipsterciso/hc-assay"
36	Issues = "https://github.com/thehipsterciso/hc-assay/issues"
37	
38	[project.optional-dependencies]
39	dev = [
40	    "pytest>=8",

The response body was not retrieved. If this URL requires authentication, use an authenticated tool (e.g. `gh` for GitHub, or an MCP-provided fetch tool) instead of WebFetch.

content.text:
Confirmed. The package is not on PyPI (404 via both WebFetch and curl), and the version is `0.0.1` (never published). The README leads the Quickstart with `pip install assay-engine` as the primary, uncaveated install path, while CONTRIBUTING.md correctly uses `pip install -e ".[dev]"`.

Let me verify the source install actually works and confirm there's truly no caveat near the pip line.

input.command: cd /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay && ls examples/minimal_study.py 2>/dev/null && timeout 90 .venv/bin/python examples/minimal_study.py >/tmp/ex.out 2>&1; echo "exit=$?"; tail -5 /tmp/ex.out; echo "=== import check ==="; .venv/bin/python -c "import assay_engine; print('installed version:', assay_engine.__version__)" 2>&1 | tail -2

content.text:
All findings confirmed. The example runs successfully from source (exit 0), but there is no caveat anywhere in the README about the package not being on PyPI or how to install from source. The two "clone" mentions in the README are conceptual (cloning the methodology onto a dataset), not install instructions.

My assessment:

The claim is factually correct and a genuine onboarding defect, but it is correctly calibrated at LOW severity:

- **Confirmed:** `pip install assay-engine` returns HTTP 404 (verified via WebFetch and curl). The package name is `assay-engine` (pyproject.toml:6), version `0.0.1`, never published.
- **Confirmed:** README.md:24-25 leads the Quickstart with `pip install assay-engine` / `pip install "assay-engine[all]"` as the primary, uncaveated install path. There is zero caveat in the README (no "PyPI", "not yet", "from source", "editable", or "published" language anywhere).
- **Confirmed contrast:** CONTRIBUTING.md:15,17 correctly uses `pip install -e ".[dev]"` — so the project knows the working install is from source.
- **Confirmed the methodology demo is fine:** `examples/minimal_study.py` runs to completion (exit 0, scorecard + 20 provenance entries) from the editable source install.

This is a real docs-vs-reality drift: a public reader copy-pasting the headline command gets a 404 and cannot install. For a Beta-classified, public-facing repo this is an onboarding over-claim, not merely style. But impact is limited — the very next block in the same Quickstart shows the runnable `python examples/minimal_study.py` path, the source install works, and a competent user lands on it quickly. No security, correctness, data-integrity, or runtime impact. LOW is the right severity.

This is not in the pass-1 fixed range (#101-#122) — those single-source the version (#120) but did not address the unpublished-package install claim.

input.reasoning:
CONFIRMED as a genuine docs-vs-reality drift / onboarding over-claim, correctly calibrated at LOW.

Facts verified independently:
1. README.md:23-26 leads the Quickstart with `pip install assay-engine` and `pip install "assay-engine[all]"` as the PRIMARY, uncaveated install path (read directly).
2. Distribution name is `assay-engine` (pyproject.toml:6), version `0.0.1` (src/assay_engine/__init__.py:28), Development Status :: 4 - Beta (pyproject.toml:16).
3. The package is NOT on PyPI: https://pypi.org/pypi/assay-engine/json returns HTTP 404 via both WebFetch and `curl -o /dev/null -w %{http_code}` (404). A user copy-pasting the headline command cannot install the package.
4. No caveat exists anywhere in README: grep for pypi/not yet/from source/clone/editable/published finds only conceptual "clone the methodology onto a dataset" mentions, no install caveat and no from-source instruction.
5. CONTRIBUTING.md:15,17 correctly uses the working `pip install -e ".[dev]"` / `[dev,all]` — so the project already knows the real install path is from source; the README simply doesn't reflect it.
6. The methodology demo is unaffected: examples/minimal_study.py runs to completion from the editable source install (exit 0, scorecard alignment_rate=1.0, 20 verified provenance entries).

Why LOW (not higher): real but low impact. No security/correctness/data-integrity/runtime effect — purely a documentation onboarding friction. Mitigated by the same Quickstart immediately showing the working `python examples/minimal_study.py` path and the source install functioning; a competent reader recovers quickly. Not a false positive, not already handled (no caveat present), and not in the pass-1 fixed range #101-#122 (those single-sourced the version via #120 but never addressed the unpublished-package install claim — so this is a gap they missed, not a regression).
input.evidence:
PyPI 404 (two independent checks): WebFetch on https://pypi.org/pypi/assay-engine/json -> "HTTP 404 Not Found"; curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" same URL -> 404.

README.md:23-26 (verbatim):
```
pip install assay-engine            # dependency-free core
pip install "assay-engine[all]"     # + every optional backend
```
No caveat: `grep -ni "pypi|not yet|from source|editable|published" README.md` returns nothing relevant (only conceptual "clone onto a dataset" prose).

pyproject.toml:6 `name = "assay-engine"`; :16 `Development Status :: 4 - Beta`. Version src/assay_engine/__init__.py:28 `__version__ = "0.0.1"`.

Working path exists but undocumented in README: CONTRIBUTING.md:15 `pip install -e ".[dev]"`, :17 `pip install -e ".[dev,all]"`. Source install confirmed functional: `.venv/bin/python examples/minimal_study.py` -> exit 0, "scorecard: supported=1 ... alignment_rate=1.0", "provenance entries: 20 (chain verified before return)"; `import assay_engine; assay_engine.__version__` -> 0.0.1.

Suggested fix (matches assessor): add a Quickstart note that assay-engine is not yet on PyPI and show the from-source install (clone + `pip install -e .`), matching CONTRIBUTING.md, or mark the pip line as the post-publication path until the package is published.
